OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: run_manifest.json stores repo_root and run_dir via java.io.File.getCanonicalPath(), which can normalize segment casing (e.g. results\switching\runs vs results\Switching\runs) compared to MATLAB fullfile() strings.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: String identity between manifest paths and MATLAB fullfile paths for the same run (case-preserved).

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: execution_status.csv is overwritten multiple times during a single run (PARTIAL then PARTIAL then SUCCESS or FAILED). Readers who copy the file mid-run see non-final EXECUTION_STATUS.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: A single immutable final row or append-only log for status (contract often interpreted as file-at-end).

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: After restoredefaultpath and addpath, script displays pwd; under run() the current folder is typically the script directory (e.g. .../Switching/analysis), not the repository root.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Documentation or operators may assume cwd equals repo root for relative resolution.

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: Raw data directory is parsed from legacy Switching_main.m and may point outside the repo (e.g. cloud-synced user paths). Science and reproducibility depend on that external tree.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Canonical contract in-repo only; raw paths are environment-specific.

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: If run_dir is unset, catch tries createRunContext(..., switching_canonical_failure), then on failure creates results/Switching/runs/run_failure_<timestamp> without manifest path.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Single deterministic failure output path aligned with normal createRunContext manifest lifecycle.

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: If run_dir is unknown or missing, markers append to tables/runtime_execution_markers_fallback.txt (repo tables/, not run_dir).
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: All execution observability confined to the active run_dir (Phase 3.7 reduced pre-run use; fallback still exists).

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: run_switching_canonical_implementation_status.csv and reports carry verdict columns (e.g. CANONICAL_PIPELINE_CONFIRMED) that can be read as pass/fail alongside execution_status.csv SUCCESS.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: execution_status.csv is the only authoritative final outcome; other files are diagnostic (partially documented in script).

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: run_manifest.json embeds git_commit, host, user, matlab_version — identity and environment, not a pure deterministic build fingerprint.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Strict reproducibility fingerprint vs run identity record (docs: infrastructure_laws).

LOCATION: repo root load.m vs MATLAB builtin
OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: addpath(repoRoot) before tools can shadow builtin load with repo load.m; MATLAB may warn: Function load has the same name as a MATLAB built-in.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: No name collision with builtins on the path for canonical runs.

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: On guard failure, appends to tables/pre_execution_failure_log.csv under repo (not under any run_dir).
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Failure artifacts only in run-scoped locations (guard is infra-wide by design).

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: Registry lists canonical path; runtime does not re-validate this file. A moved or renamed script could diverge from the table.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Registry automatically enforced at execution time.

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: If fileList is empty for a Temp Dep folder, the code continues to the next folder without surfacing a per-folder warning in execution outputs.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Explicit visibility when a folder under parentDir produces no processable files.

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: If Tvec is empty after filters, continue skips contributing rows without logging that folder as skipped.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Explicit audit trail for skipped raw segments.

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: Rules list execution_probe_top.txt, execution_status.csv, run_dir as validity; wrapper exit code is explicitly not a validity indicator. Operators may still use MATLAB exit code from habit.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Operators rely only on signaling contract artifacts.

OBSERVED_BEHAVIOR: run_id embeds local clock (yyyy_MM_dd_HHmmss); collisions resolved with suffix; not derivable from inputs alone.
If SCRIPT_ARG is omitted, PowerShell resolves SCRIPT_PATH_RESOLVED to current directory (.), not an .m file; guard exits 2 (correct), but echo lines can look like a valid path intent.
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: Explicit error when no script argument (optional UX).
